compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container review (WS2 child vs parent + full sibling roster). Findings across the 7 cross-child interaction modes:

**[G4 / dependency-gap + ordering-violation] WS2 carries the Sonnet-downgrade replacement that WS1's `verifier_cfg` deletion depends on, but NO dependency edge encodes WS1-after-WS2 (and it tensions with the parent's "WS1 lands FIRST" sequencing).**
- criteria: WS2 AC "The workflow verify step pins the decisive non-frontier verifier model (Sonnet) by default ... via the existing per-step `model:` / `resolve_model` path"; WS1 AC "Once parity passes, `_run_passes`, `pass2_verify`, and `verifier_cfg` are deleted"; parent Design-decisions "WS1 (migration) lands first."
- location: WS2 deps = [] ; WS1 (80c3) description NOTE ("the deletion of `verifier_cfg` here should land after WS2 so the workflow verify path does not lose the downgrade") + WS1 AC bullet 5; parent sequencing prose.
- evidence: (1) Verified `verifier_cfg` (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:693) downgrades to `claude-sonnet-4-6` only when `cfg.model == DEFAULT_MODEL` — the override-preserving semantics WS2 reproduces. (2) Verified the workflow verify step (plan-review.yaml lines 133 & 190) carries NO `model:` today and `resolve_model` (config.py:72, used in runs.py:124) would fall back to `cfg.model` (the runner default/Opus). (3) WS2 deps = []; WS1 deps = []. The only dep edges in the roster are WS3→WS1 and WS3→WS2. The WS1-after-WS2 ordering that protects the downgrade lives ONLY as prose in WS1's NOTE, not as an enforceable edge on either ticket.
- scenarios: Per the parent's "WS1 lands first," WS1 is claimed/landed first and deletes `verifier_cfg` before WS2 pins the per-step `model:` on the verify step → the workflow verify path silently reverts to the runner default (Opus), losing the decisive non-frontier downgrade — exactly the regression the epic's Success Criteria forbids ("applies the Sonnet-by-default verifier model").
- impact: Following the declared dependency graph permits the downgrade-dropping ordering; the only safeguard is unenforceable prose split across WS1.
- checklist_item: dependency gap; ordering violation
- suggested_fix: Encode the WS1-after-WS2 constraint on the `verifier_cfg` deletion explicitly — e.g. gate WS1's `verifier_cfg` deletion on WS2 landing (an edge or a split-out deletion sub-step), and reconcile the parent prose to state precisely: WS1 migration + `_run_passes`/`pass2_verify` deletion lands first, but the `verifier_cfg` deletion is gated on WS2 pinning the per-step model. (Mirror of the finding already noted on WS1; recorded here because WS2 is the half that provides the replacement yet carries no edge.)

**[G4 / consumer-impact + conflicting-assumptions] WS2 says "the verify step" (singular) but there are TWO verify steps (agentic + single-turn branches); WS3 sizes against "the verify step's resolved model" per branch, so a one-branch pin yields divergent resolved models.**
- criteria: WS2 AC "The workflow verify step pins ... (Sonnet)"; WS3 (4a0e) AC "A token estimate ... decides verify chunking ... against the verify step's resolved model window"; WS3 oversized-finding AC escalates "from the resolved verify model."
- location: plan-review.yaml verify steps at line 133 (agentic branch, prompt `plan-review-verifier-agentic`) and line 190 (single-turn branch, prompt `plan-review-verifier`), each `id: verify`.
- evidence: (1) Both branches contain an independent `verify` step with no `model:` today. (2) WS4 (679b) explicitly scopes its coach gate to "both the agentic and single-turn branches," demonstrating the two-branch shape is a known, must-address concern in this workflow. (3) WS2's What/AC reference "the verify step" in the singular and never call out the agentic-vs-single-turn split, unlike WS4. (4) WS3 depends_on WS2 and consumes "the resolved verify model" as the budget/escalation base.
- scenarios: WS2 pins `model:` on only one verify branch (the singular AC reading). Then for the unpinned branch the resolved verify model stays the runner default; WS3's budget and `models_at_or_above(resolved_verify_model)` escalation base differ between branches → inconsistent chunking/escalation behavior, and the Sonnet downgrade is dropped on whichever branch runs in that mode.
- impact: A partial pin both (a) regresses the downgrade Success Criterion on one branch and (b) hands WS3 an ambiguous/divergent resolved-model base — the very "no two divergent rules" hazard the epic exists to remove.
- checklist_item: consumer impact; conflicting assumptions
- suggested_fix: Make WS2 explicit that the per-step `model:` pin (and BPMN round-trip) applies to BOTH verify branches (agentic + single-turn), matching WS4's "both branches" scoping, so the resolved verify model WS3 sizes against is unambiguous across branches.

**[G4 / scope-overlap] plan-review.yaml co-edited by WS1, WS2, WS4 — benign (disjoint regions), noted for sequencing awareness only.**
- criteria: WS2 file_impact `plan-review.yaml` (verify-step model:); WS1 (probe-mode/criteria subset); WS4 (`when: surviving>0` on coach_notes).
- evidence: WS2 touches the `verify` step (lines 133/190); WS4 touches `coach_notes` (lines 159/216); WS1 adds probe-mode (criteria allowlist or separate `drift-probe.yaml`). Distinct YAML regions; no overlapping keys.
- impact: Low — no contradiction. Pure merge-coordination note if landed in parallel.
- checklist_item: scope overlap
- suggested_fix: None required; ordinary merge coordination.

No other modes detected. Implicit-shared-state: WS2's `runs.py`/`config.py resolve_model` + `bpmn.py`/`editor.py` are not edited by any sibling — no shared-state hazard. Residual-references: verified `verifier_cfg` and `resolve_model` references resolve correctly today; WS2 introduces the per-step model surface that WS1's deletion relies on — no dangling reference introduced by WS2 itself.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container review (WS2 child vs parent + sibling roster). Findings:

**[G4 / dependency-gap + ordering-violation] WS2 is the producer of the Sonnet-downgrade replacement that WS1's `verifier_cfg` deletion depends on, but no dependency edge encodes this from either side, and it conflicts with the parent's "WS1 lands FIRST" prose.**
- The downgrade-preservation safeguard exists only as prose in WS1's NOTE ("the deletion of `verifier_cfg` here should land after WS2 so the workflow verify path does not lose the downgrade"); WS2 (this ticket) has deps=[] and says nothing about being a prerequisite for WS1's deletion. Per the declared graph, WS1 (which the parent says lands FIRST) could ship the `verifier_cfg` deletion before WS2 pins the per-step `model:` Sonnet downgrade → the workflow verify path silently reverts to the runner default (Opus), losing the decisive non-frontier downgrade the epic explicitly wants preserved (parent Success Criteria: "applies the Sonnet-by-default verifier model").
- This is the same hazard already filed on WS1 (80c3), viewed from WS2's side: WS2 produces the declarative replacement; nothing makes WS1's deletion wait for it. Suggested fix: encode the ordering as an enforceable edge (gate WS1's `verifier_cfg`-deletion step on WS2 landing) rather than leaving it as cross-ticket prose, OR reconcile the parent sequencing to state the precise order (WS1 migration + `_run_passes`/`pass2_verify` deletion first, `verifier_cfg` deletion gated on WS2's per-step model pin).
- criteria: parent Success "workflow verify step applies the Sonnet-by-default verifier model"; parent AC WS2 "pins the decisive non-frontier verifier model (Sonnet) by default"; WS1 AC "Once parity passes, ... `verifier_cfg` are deleted".
- checklist_item: dependency gap; ordering violation
- suggested_fix: add an explicit edge so WS1's verifier_cfg deletion is gated on WS2's verify-step model pin (or split that deletion out), and align the parent's "WS1 first" prose with this precise ordering.

**[G4 / scope-overlap on plan-review.yaml — verify step] benign co-edit, dep-encoded one direction only; flagged for awareness.**
- WS2 edits the verify step in `plan-review.yaml` to pin `model: Sonnet`; WS1 edits the same file (probe-mode / criteria subset) and also touches the verify path's return shape / model semantics; WS3 sizes against the verify step's resolved `model:` WS2 pins. WS3→WS2 dep edge exists (consistent). WS1↔WS2 both touch the verify step (WS1 removes `verifier_cfg`'s downgrade, WS2 reintroduces it declaratively on the same step) — the regions interact (the very downgrade WS1 drops is what WS2 restores), which is the substance of the ordering finding above, not a separate merge-conflict-only overlap.
- impact: Low as a pure file overlap; the material risk is the ordering one above.
- checklist_item: scope overlap; consumer impact
- suggested_fix: none beyond the ordering fix above.

No other modes detected: WS2 docs/editor/BPMN work (`bpmn.py`, `editor.py`, docs) is disjoint from sibling touch-sets (no shared-state, no conflicting assumption, no residual reference). Parent WS2 acceptance criteria (pin+override, properties-panel edit, BPMN round-trip, legacy back-compat, docs) are each carried by this child 1:1 — no coverage gap.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 8 advisory). Triage + concrete decisions (remediating the valid advisories in implementation rather than churning the attested description):
- [T8 f91b33 — the key one] A static per-step `model: sonnet` CANNOT honor an operator override because resolve_model precedence is step>workflow>cfg (a literal step model always wins). So the verifier Sonnet DEFAULT is applied via cfg-tuning at the review_plan() library entry — mirroring verify_completion (completion.py:200): `if cfg.model == DEFAULT_MODEL: cfg = replace(cfg, model=VERIFIER_DEFAULT_MODEL)`. This honors REBAR_LLM_MODEL exactly like verifier_cfg. The per-step `model:` editor field is a SEPARATE general capability that, when explicitly set on a step, wins via resolve_model — the two compose.
- [fa622c/f87a8b] Concrete model id = claude-sonnet-4-6 (VERIFIER_DEFAULT_MODEL, promoted to config.py as a shared const so completion.py + plan_review share one source — no duplication).
- [T8 f40b68] Editor model-select vocabulary = sizing.MODEL_LADDER ids (claude-haiku-4-5, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-8) + blank=inherit. Single source: sizing.MODEL_LADDER.
- [E5 fb84a8] Add a test asserting the resolved verify model is Sonnet by default and the operator's model when REBAR_LLM_MODEL is set.
- [G5 f45d8d/fd93d3] Thin vertical slice / de-risk order: (1) backend Sonnet pin + test (the load-bearing behavior) FIRST, then (2) editor model field + BPMN round-trip/back-compat tests (per-step model already round-trips via <rebar:Config>; legacy BPMN degrades gracefully), then (3) docs. WS2 is already a decomposed child of the epic.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The bespoke `verifier_cfg` downgrades the verify pass from the default frontier model (Opus) to a
decisive non-frontier model (Sonnet) unless the operator explicitly chose a model. The workflow verify
step does NOT apply this today (it uses the runner default), so migrating drift-refresh off bespoke
(WS1) would lose the downgrade. Reproduce it declaratively via the existing per-step `model:` key
(`resolve_model`, WS-D3: step > workflow > cfg). Because this makes a per-step `model:` key first-class
on a shipped gate workflow, it must also be reflected in the visual editor UX — today only the
workflow-level model is carried in BPMN, so editing the workflow in the visual editor could silently
drop a step's model.

## What

- Pin the workflow verify step's `model:` to the decisive non-frontier verifier (Sonnet) by default,
  resolved via the existing per-step `model:` / `resolve_model` path; honor an explicit operator model
  override (parity with `verifier_cfg`).
- Visual editor: surface the per-step `model` field in the properties panel (a select over the known
  model ladder), and make a per-step `model` round-trip through BPMN export/import so a visual edit +
  save never drops it.
- BPMN back-compat: importing a pre-existing BPMN document that lacks the per-step model attribute
  defaults gracefully to "no per-step override" (the step falls back to the workflow/cfg model via
  `resolve_model`), never errors.
- Document the per-step `model:` knob in the workflow-authoring + editor docs.

## Scope

In scope: the verify-step model pin + override, the editor properties-panel field, BPMN round-trip +
back-compat, and docs. Out of scope: drift-refresh migration / bespoke deletion (WS1), token chunking
(WS3 sizes against the model this story pins), the coach-gate nit (WS4). Touches:
`src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml`, `runs.py` (`resolve_model`), `bpmn.py`, `editor.py`,
and the workflow-authoring/editor docs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The workflow verify step pins the decisive non-frontier verifier model (Sonnet) by default,
      resolved via the existing per-step `model:` / `resolve_model` path.
- [ ] An explicit operator model override is honored on the verify step (parity with `verifier_cfg`).
- [ ] The visual editor properties panel lets a user view/edit a step's `model`.
- [ ] A per-step `model` survives a BPMN export → import (visual-edit → save) round-trip unchanged; a
      regression test asserts the round-trip.
- [ ] Importing a legacy BPMN document that lacks the per-step model attribute succeeds and falls back
      to the workflow/cfg model (no error, no per-step override).
- [ ] Authoring + editor docs document the per-step `model:` knob.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.
compiled_state.title: WS2: Per-step verifier model:key (Sonnet downgrade) surfaced in the visual editor UX
